segments.text: A Michtam of David, for teaching, when he struggled with Aram-naharaim and Aram-zobah, and Joab returned and struck down twelve thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
O God, you have rejected us, broken our defenses; you have been angry; now restore us!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You have made the land tremble; you have torn it open; repair its breaches, for it totters.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You have made your people see hard things; you have given us wine to drink that made us stagger.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You have set up a banner for those who fear you, that they may flee to it from the bow.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: That your beloved ones may be delivered, give salvation by your right hand and answer us!
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: God has spoken in his holiness: 'I will rejoice, I will divide up Shechem and portion out the Valley of Succoth.'
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Gilead is mine; Manasseh is mine; Ephraim is my helmet; Judah is my scepter;
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: triumph…: or, triumph thou over me: (by an irony)

segments.text: Moab is my washbasin; over Edom I cast my shoe; over Philistia I shout in triumph.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Who will bring me to the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Have you not rejected us, O God? You do not go out, O God, with our armies.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: help of man: Heb. salvation, etc

segments.text: Oh, grant us help against the foe, for the help of man is worthless.
segments.speaker: David
